default_creds.pass: (no password - hardcoded test user returned when AUTHENTICATE=false)
default_creds.context: auth_bearer.py get_test_user(): id=39418e3b-0258-4452-af60-7acfcc1263ff. Returned for EVERY request when AUTHENTICATE env=false.
Default JWT_SECRET_KEY in .env.example = public Supabase demo secret. Sign any HS256 token with role:authenticated and a sub/user_id to pass jwt_token_handler.verify_token (verify_aud:False).

install_tell: Legacy full-stack (internet-exposed population): git clone https://github.com/QuivrHQ/quivr && cp .env.example .env && docker compose pull && docker compose up (frontend:3000, backend-api:5050, flower:5555, redis:6379). Current library: pip install quivr-core (no server).

response_markers: GET / on :5050 -> FastAPI default; OpenAPI at /docs and /openapi.json lists vendor-unique routers: brain_router, chat_router, crawl_router, assistant_router, sync_router, onboarding_router, knowledge_router, api_key_router
GET /user/identity or /chat with no token -> 401 {"detail":...} when AUTHENTICATE=true (gated), 200 when AUTHENTICATE=false (open admin)
frontend :3000 HTML contains NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL / NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL Next.js env injection and 'Quivr' title
active_probe.false_positive_check: 'Quivr' and 'chat'/'AI' collide widely; do NOT fingerprint on those alone. Confirm the STACK SHAPE: (1) FastAPI/uvicorn on 5050 whose /openapi.json enumerates the vendor-unique router set brain+crawl+assistant+knowledge+onboarding+api_key together (no other product has that exact combo), AND (2) a Next.js frontend on 3000 that injects NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_* + NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL pointing at :5050. The Supabase anon key alone is shared across every Supabase app, so it is NOT discriminating on its own. The /openapi.json router-name set is the load-bearing discriminator. quivr-core (the new PyPI library) has no listener, so any live HTTP host is by definition the legacy full-stack app.

deployment_tells: docker-compose container_name set: web, backend-api, worker, notifier, beat, flower, redis
backend image stangirard/quivr-backend-prebuilt:latest; frontend image quivr-frontend-prebuilt
Ports 3000 (Next.js front), 5050 (FastAPI uvicorn backend, /healthz), 5555 (Celery Flower), 6379 (Redis) co-located on one host
Celery + Redis broker (CELERY_BROKER_URL redis://redis:6379/0, queue quivr-preview.fifo)
FastAPI /openapi.json router set unique to Quivr (brain/crawl/assistant/knowledge/onboarding/api_key)
extra_hosts host.docker.internal:host-gateway pattern pointing Supabase at :54321/:54322
TELEMETRY_ENABLED=true default beacons to Quivr (PostHog/sentry hooks present)
pivot_paths: GET :5050/openapi.json and /docs (FastAPI) to enumerate full route surface unauthenticated regardless of AUTHENTICATE
GET :5050/healthz to confirm liveness without auth
If AUTHENTICATE=false: GET /user/identity, /chat, /brain, /knowledge as the implicit admin@quivr.app with no token (read brains, chats, uploaded docs)

If AUTHENTICATE=true but default JWT secret unchanged: forge HS256 token signed with 'super-secret-jwt-token-with-at-least-32-characters-long' (role:authenticated, arbitrary sub) and read any user's brains/chats - read-only confirm via /user/identity echo
Flower :5555 dashboard - read Celery task args/results (may leak file paths, brain ids, doc names) with no auth
Front-end :3000 JS bundle - extract NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL/ANON_KEY + NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL to confirm Supabase project and backend location
If self-hosted Supabase reachable: PostgREST on :54321 with the demo service_role key = full DB read
vulnerabilities: CVE-2024-9229 (CVSS 7.5 HIGH): Unauthenticated DoS via crafted multipart boundary in the file-upload endpoint causes uncontrolled resource consumption (CWE-400). Affected: stangirard/quivr v0.0.298.
CVE-2024-6583: Path traversal in upload allows writing files to arbitrary paths in the backing S3 bucket via '..' in the upload path. Affected: latest stangirard/quivr at disclosure.
CVE-2024-4851: SSRF in the /crawl endpoint ('url' param in backend/routes/crawl_routes.py) lets an attacker reach internal networks. Affected: stangirard/quivr v0.0.204.

Design weakness (not a CVE): default .env.example ships the public Supabase demo JWT_SECRET_KEY and demo service/anon keys -> forgeable HS256 auth tokens; AUTHENTICATE=false returns a hardcoded admin@quivr.app for all requests; Flower (5555) exposed without auth in default compose.
auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: default .env.example at tag v0.0.317 (commit 3523665) sets `AUTHENTICATE=true`, so the legacy full-stack app (frontend:3000 + FastAPI backend-api:5050, both `ports:`-exposed in the default docker-compose.yml) does gate the API behind a Supabase HS256 JWT by default. BUT the same .env.example ships the publicly-known Supabase local-dev secrets verbatim: `JWT_SECRET_KEY=super-secret-jwt-token-with-at-least-32-characters-long`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY=eyJ...role:service_role iss:supabase-demo`, and `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJ...role:anon iss:supabase-demo` (the exact documented Supabase demo keys). backend/api/quivr_api/middlewares/auth/jwt_token_handler.py decodes with `jwt.decode(token, SECRET_KEY, algorithms=['HS256'], options={'verify_aud': False})` reading SECRET_KEY from JWT_SECRET_KEY. So an operator who runs the quickstart unchanged exposes a backend whose JWT secret is a public constant: any attacker can forge an HS256 token for any user_id and pass auth (aud unverified). Separately, auth_bearer.py honors `AUTHENTICATE=false` by returning a hardcoded admin (`UserIdentity(email='admin@quivr.app', id='39418e3b-0258-4452-af60-7acfcc1263ff')`) for every request with no credentials. The Celery Flower monitor (port 5555) ships with no auth in the default compose. Posture is configurable-default-on in name, but the shipped default secret renders it effectively forgeable-unauth. NOTE: current repo `main` is now the `quivr-core` PyPI library (`pip install quivr-core`) which is a code library with NO server/listener/auth surface; the internet-exposed population is the pre-refactor full-stack app.
sensitive_assets: Upstream LLM API keys (OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, MISTRAL_API_KEY, GROQ_API_KEY, COHERE_API_KEY, BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY) held server-side in .env and reachable via chat/model routes
Ingested RAG documents ('brains') and their chunked text / embeddings - user-uploaded knowledge base content, potential PII/business docs
Conversation/chat history per brain (chat_router) - prompts and model answers
Brain/assistant/workflow definitions revealing business logic (brain_router, assistant_router, retrieval_config_workflow.yaml)
Supabase Postgres + pgvector store (PG_DATABASE_URL postgres:postgres) holding users, brains, vectors
Google Drive / SharePoint / Notion sync OAuth client secrets (GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, SHAREPOINT_CLIENT_ID) and synced tenant documents (sync_router)
API keys minted via api_key_router; SMTP/Resend credentials
sources: [CANDIDATE - NuClide Cat-34 rag-app-builders Stage -1 OSINT 2026-06-12; software-default characterization, no live host probed yet]
